response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but the fit for this role is weak. His C++ experience is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure (RIDE Capital was a retail investment fintech, not capital markets), and his SQL background lists MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle. His recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web/platform work, which does not substitute for the specialized profile required.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Good communication skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old, with no evident Windows-platform C++ focus
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
